panels.title: Wraparound risk — top-N tables (drill down to specific tables)

panels.description: Top-N tables driving the database-level XID wraparound clock — `age(pg_class.relfrozenxid)`. Lines climbing here mean specific tables are not getting frozen; a single laggard can dominate the database `datfrozenxid`. Threshold reference lines at `autovacuum_freeze_max_age` (200M) and the failsafe range (1.6B+).
panels.title: Top-N tables by XID age (relfrozenxid)

panels.description: Top-N tables driving the database-level MultiXID wraparound clock — `mxid_age(pg_class.relminmxid)`. New per-table metric `pgwatch_table_stats_mxid_freeze_age` (added alongside `tx_freeze_age` in `table_stats`). Threshold reference lines: `autovacuum_multixact_freeze_max_age` (soft, 400M default), `vacuum_multixact_failsafe_age` (failsafe, PG14+ only, hidden when 0), and a static ~2^31 − 10M hard limit (cluster refuses new MultiXIDs).
panels.title: Top-N tables by MultiXID age (relminmxid)

panels.description: Data and catalog xmin horizon ages with source component ages, measured in transaction age.
panels.title: xmin horizon age by source
Longest non-idle transaction age, > 1 min
panels.description: Longest currently observed non-idle transaction age for the selected database.
How many current blockers exist in each xmin horizon source, with slot xmin and catalog_xmin tracked separately.
panels.title: Current blocker counts

panels.description: Per-table autovacuum overdue factor: dead_tup divided by the per-relation trigger threshold (autovacuum_vacuum_threshold + autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor * reltuples, with pg_class.reloptions overrides applied). 1.0 = exactly at the trigger; >1.0 = overdue (autovacuum should run / has been picked but hasn't completed); <1.0 = not yet eligible. Sort desc to triage tables that autovacuum is failing to keep up with.
panels.title: Autovacuum debt — top-N overdue tables

panels.description: Active autovacuum workers vs the configured pool size (autovacuum_max_workers). Pinning at the cap is a leading indicator of bloat / wraparound risk that progress-only panels miss — when active_workers stays at max_workers, the autovacuum scheduler is saturated and tables are queued.
panels.title: Autovacuum worker pool — active vs max

panels.description: Autovacuum workers currently waiting on a lock, joined to the lock-holder PID and queryid. An anti-wraparound autovacuum stuck behind DDL is a silent XID-eater. Look up the holder's user/application_name in pg_stat_activity by blocker_pid when needed (intentionally not exposed as Prometheus labels for cardinality / info-disclosure reasons). The queryid drilldown link is empty on PG <14 (no pg_stat_activity.query_id column). Empty panel = good news (see "no data" message).

defaults.noValue: no autovacuum workers waiting on locks

value.title: View query analysis (PG14+; empty on older versions)

panels.title: Autovacuum workers blocked on lock

targets.expr:
(
    group by (schema_name, table_name, vacuum_mode) (
        pgwatch_pg_vacuum_progress_index_vacuum_count{phase="7", schema_name=~"$schema_name", table_name=~"$table_name"}) * 0 + 7 or 
    group by (schema_name, table_name, vacuum_mode) (
        pgwatch_pg_vacuum_progress_index_vacuum_count{phase="6", schema_name=~"$schema_name", table_name=~"$table_name"}) * 0 + 6 or
    group by (schema_name, table_name, vacuum_mode) (
        pgwatch_pg_vacuum_progress_index_vacuum_count{phase="5", schema_name=~"$schema_name", table_name=~"$table_name"}) * 0 + 5 or 
    group by (schema_name, table_name, vacuum_mode) (
        pgwatch_pg_vacuum_progress_index_vacuum_count{phase="4", schema_name=~"$schema_name", table_name=~"$table_name"}) * 0 + 4 or 
    group by (schema_name, table_name, vacuum_mode) (
        pgwatch_pg_vacuum_progress_index_vacuum_count{phase="3", schema_name=~"$schema_name", table_name=~"$table_name"}) * 0 + 3 or 
    group by (schema_name, table_name, vacuum_mode) (
        pgwatch_pg_vacuum_progress_index_vacuum_count{phase="2", schema_name=~"$schema_name", table_name=~"$table_name"}) * 0 + 2 or 
    group by (schema_name, table_name, vacuum_mode) (
        pgwatch_pg_vacuum_progress_index_vacuum_count{phase="1", schema_name=~"$schema_name", table_name=~"$table_name"}) * 0 + 1
)

title: 07. Autovacuum and xmin horizon
description: Autovacuum activity, long-running transaction age, xmin horizon pressure, and per-database / per-table wraparound risk (XID and MultiXID).
